era_context: The Middle Bronze Age II in the Levant and Egypt; the 12th Dynasty of Egypt under Amenemhat III. In Mesopotamia, Hammurabi's Old Babylonian state is consolidating power. The Indus Valley Civilization is in its mature phase. In China, the Xia Dynasty (traditional dates) is in its later period.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Sub-Saharan Africa: no written records; archaeological evidence is limited and unevenly distributed.
The Americas: no written records; archaeological context is not recoverable at annual resolution.
Northern Europe and the Steppe: sparse written documentation; primarily archaeological inference.
Southeast Asia and Oceania: minimal written records; archaeological evidence is fragmentary.

events.title: Amenemhat III: continued reign and administrative activity in Egypt
events.description: Amenemhat III's reign continues with documented administrative activity, building projects, and economic prosperity. Inscriptions record mining expeditions, trade with the Levant, and the maintenance of Egypt's borders. The Fayum region continues to be developed through irrigation and settlement. Egypt's centralized bureaucratic state remains stable and prosperous.

sources.name: Temple inscriptions and administrative records from the 12th Dynasty

events.certainty_note: Amenemhat III's reign is well-attested. The year -1962 falls within his documented reign; specific annual events are rarely distinguishable.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. However, the chronological placement of this year remains subject to the broader uncertainties affecting the dating of the 12th Dynasty and Hammurabi's reign.
historiographic_note: Documentation for this year is minimal. Written records are limited to administrative and religious texts from Egypt and Mesopotamia. The archaeological record provides material evidence but cannot resolve events to annual precision. Most of the world's regions have no written documentation for this period.

graph_edges.note: Amenemhat III's reign continues; part of the same period of Egyptian prosperity.
